changes.check: Source cross-reference — D-1 output fields available for D-2 consumption
changes.detail: ConsumerDomainScoreV1 fields available to D-2 narrative assembly: domain_id, consumer_label, clinical_label, score (0-1), band_label (strong/stable/watch/review), confidence_tier (high/medium/low), active_signal_ids, primary_idl_record_id (nullable), missing_marker_ids, source_track, caveat_flags, contributing_system_keys, raw_evidence_refs (contains layer3_system_pressure_id per domain). All three domains emit these fields deterministically. IMPORTANT: ConsumerDomainScoreV1 has NO narrative sentence fields yet — D-2 must extend this Pydantic model with Optional narrative fields declared as proper Pydantic fields (frozen=True, extra=forbid constraint applies at results.py:172).

changes.check: Source cross-reference — IDL YAML cardiovascular gap status
changes.result: CONFIRMED — GAP ALREADY RESOLVED IN YAML — ROUTING FIX ONLY NEEDED
changes.detail: CRITICAL FINDING. ph_lipid_residual_ldl_favourable_transport_v1 NOW HAS a why_it_matters field in the YAML: 'Cumulative atherogenic exposure is read in full transport context; reassuring HDL does not erase residual LDL-related risk.' This directly contradicts DOMAIN_NARRATIVE_CONTRACT_WAVE1.md:49 and :638 which states 'no why_it_matters field in this record' and declares this a gap. The content already exists. D-2 must NOT author new why_it_matters content for this record — it would overwrite the existing governed text. The cardiovascular consequence fix is now a ROUTING fix only: wire the selection logic to route ph_lipid_residual_ldl_favourable_transport_v1.why_it_matters when this IDL record is primary_idl_record_id. No KB content authoring is required. If Cursor sees DOMAIN_NARRATIVE_CONTRACT_WAVE1.md and attempts to add a new why_it_matters field, it must NOT do so — the field already exists at line 113 of the YAML.

CONFIRMED. ph_hba1c_metabolic_stress_v1.why_it_matters (line 23): 'Sustained glycaemic strain raises long-term metabolic and vascular risk.' CONFIRMED. ph_vascular_hcy_inflammation_v1.why_it_matters (line 33): 'Accumulating vascular-risk signals warrant cardiometabolic review and lifestyle action.' CONFIRMED. ph_lipid_residual_ldl_favourable_transport_v1.why_it_matters (line 113): 'Cumulative atherogenic exposure is read in full transport context; reassuring HDL does not erase residual LDL-related risk.' changes.check: Source cross-reference — IDL subtitle fields for contributor sentence assembly
changes.detail: ph_metabolic_early_ir_v1.subtitle (line 12): 'A pattern suggesting early impaired sugar and lipid handling'. ph_hba1c_metabolic_stress_v1.subtitle (line 21): 'A pattern suggesting sustained blood-sugar strain on the body'. ph_vascular_hcy_inflammation_v1.subtitle (line 30): 'A pattern combining inflammation and homocysteine signals associated with vascular risk'. ph_lipid_residual_ldl_favourable_transport_v1.subtitle (line 112): 'LDL elevation alongside relatively stronger HDL signalling—particle architecture still matters'. ph_hepatic_alt_inflammatory_v1.subtitle (line 43): 'A pattern suggesting metabolic or inflammatory strain in the liver'. All subtitles confirmed present and available for contributor sentence assembly.

changes.check: Source cross-reference — Layer3InsightCard structure and system card IDs
changes.detail: cardiovascular__system_pressure, metabolic__system_pressure, hepatic__system_pressure are all in SYSTEM_CARD_IDS (line 15-27). InsightCard fields confirmed: title (line 90), severity Literal['action','watch','info'] (line 91), confidence Literal['high','medium','low'] (line 92), interpretation: str (line 94), next_steps: List[str] (line 95). CRITICAL ACCESS PATH CONSTRAINT: Layer3InsightsV1 is NOT a field on AnalysisDTO. It is assembled separately by assemble_layer3_insights(dto: AnalysisDTO) in backend/core/layer3/insight_assembler_v1.py:158. The current assemble_consumer_domain_scores_v1() signature does NOT accept Layer3InsightsV1. D-2 MUST extend the assembler to accept Layer3InsightsV1 as an additional argument (pre-assembled in orchestrator before the domain assembler call), OR create a separate narrative assembly function. Layer3InsightsV1 is the source for next_step_sentence (next_steps[0]) and contributor fallback (interpretation). This wiring does not require frontend changes and is achievable within the backend-only constraint.

changes.result: CONFIRMED — PARTIAL ACCESS; PRIMARY SIGNAL STATE AVAILABLE FROM INSIGHT GRAPH
changes.citation: backend/core/contracts/report_v1.py:85 (RootCauseV1 on ReportV1); backend/core/analytics/domain_score_assembler.py:86-94 (_iter_signal_results reads signal_state from InsightGraph)
changes.detail: RootCauseV1 lives on ReportV1.root_cause_v1, compiled by report_compiler_v1.py:769. It is NOT on AnalysisDTO and NOT currently passed to the domain assembler. However: (1) signal_state per signal is already available from InsightGraph.signal_results (as confirmed in D-1 — _iter_signal_results() iterates these). D-2 can read signal_state for contributor sentence selection from the same InsightGraph already passed to the assembler. (2) safety_class (for next_step routing) is available as a proxy via InsightCard.severity ('action'/'watch'/'info') on Layer3InsightsV1 — no RootCauseV1 needed for this. (3) RootCauseHypothesisV1.missing_data[] reason strings are NOT accessible without RootCauseV1 — but missing_marker_ids from D-1 contract covers the marker IDs. D-2 should use governed confidence sentence templates rather than constructing sentences from missing_data reason strings. RootCauseV1 hypothesis title (liver pattern differentiation) can be sourced from IDL.subtitle or InsightCard.interpretation as fallback — no RootCauseV1 needed for Wave 1 narrative assembly.

changes.check: Source cross-reference — ConfidenceTierV1 level count vs narrative contract 4-level model
changes.detail: D-1 defines confidence_tier as 3 levels (high/medium/low). The narrative contract (DOMAIN_NARRATIVE_CONTRACT_WAVE1.md) uses 4 levels (High/Good/Moderate/Limited) with distinct confidence sentences per level. Cursor must make an explicit mapping decision: either (A) map 3 D-1 tiers to 3 of the 4 narrative confidence sentences (collapsing one level), or (B) add a new narrative_confidence_level field to ConsumerDomainScoreV1 as a separate Optional field carrying the 4-level value. The mapping must be documented in code. Option B is preferred for truthfulness — it avoids information loss. Either way, the decision must be explicit and the chosen path documented in the sprint report.

changes.check: Source cross-reference — prediabetes override in analytics core
changes.result: CONFIRMED — OVERRIDE DOES NOT EXIST IN ANALYTICS CORE
changes.citation: Grep for 'prediabetes' across backend/core/analytics/ — no matches found. Only present in backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml:1612 (metadata tag) and backend/ssot/ranges.yaml:101,118 (range boundary labels) and test files.
changes.detail: DOMAIN_NARRATIVE_CONTRACT_WAVE1.md:264 describes a 'governed prediabetes override that fires when HbA1c >= 5.7% OR glucose >= 5.6 mmol/L (ADA Standards of Care 2026).' This logic is NOT implemented anywhere in backend/core/analytics/. Cursor must NOT attempt to implement a prediabetes override in D-2 — this would be inventing unsupported logic. For the blood sugar contributor sentence, use signal_state from InsightGraph.signal_results only. If signal_hba1c_high is at_risk, use the governed HbA1c contributor sentence. If signal_insulin_resistance is at_risk, use the IR contributor sentence. The prediabetes override case in the narrative contract's §2.2 priority list MUST be skipped — it references logic that does not exist. D-2 STOP condition 2 applies: if contributor sentence cannot be assembled without inventing unsupported logic, STOP and report.

Scope precisely bounded: three Wave 1 domains, narrative field assembly in domain_score_assembler.py or equivalent, ConsumerDomainScoreV1 extension, IDL routing fix, targeted tests. Explicitly out of scope: frontend, PDF, score recalibration, thyroid, kidney, second-wave domains, LLM prose.

Key regression targets: test_domain_score_assembler_v1.py (D-1 targeted tests — both must still pass with extended model), enforcement tests: test_scoring_policy_not_hardcoded.py, test_no_raw_fields_in_insight_graph.py, test_runtime_requires_insight_graph.py. 79 enforcement tests passed in D-1 — must not regress. Any ConsumerDomainScoreV1 model extension must maintain Optional/default-safe backward compatibility for these tests.

Narrative assembler is additive — it reads D-1 outputs and adds narrative fields. Score logic, confidence_tier, band_label are NOT recalculated. D-1 assembler function _bands_ and _confidence_tier_ functions must not be modified. Existing AnalysisDTO fields are unchanged. STOP if score logic needs reopening.

The sprint narrative contract doc is stale on this point. Do NOT add new why_it_matters content. Wire the routing logic to use the existing field. The consequence fix is now a routing-only change.

LAYER3 ACCESS PATH: assemble_layer3_insights(dto) returns Layer3InsightsV1. It takes a full AnalysisDTO. The domain assembler currently takes scoring_result/insight_graph/idl_bundle/derived_ratios_meta/panel_biomarker_ids. D-2 must either (A) extend the assembler to accept an Optional[Layer3InsightsV1] parameter (pre-assembled in orchestrator before domain assembler call), or (B) create a separate narrative assembly pass. next_step_sentence and contributor fallback depend on InsightCard from Layer3.

PREDIABETES OVERRIDE DOES NOT EXIST: No prediabetes override logic exists in backend/core/analytics/. DOMAIN_NARRATIVE_CONTRACT_WAVE1.md §2.2 references this as if it exists — it does not. Blood sugar contributor sentence must use signal_state from InsightGraph only. Do not implement override logic.

CONFIDENCETIERV1 MISMATCH: D-1 confidence_tier is 3 levels (high/medium/low). Narrative contract uses 4 levels. Add a new Optional narrative_confidence_level field OR explicitly collapse 4 → 3 levels with documentation. Decision must be explicit and reported.

CONSUMER DOMAIN SCORE EXTENSION: ConsumerDomainScoreV1 is frozen=True, extra=forbid. New narrative fields (headline_sentence, contributor_sentence, confidence_sentence, consequence_sentence, next_step_sentence) must be declared as proper Optional[str] Pydantic fields with default=None (or default='') for backward compatibility. Do not inject as extra kwargs.

SIGNAL STATE ACCESS: signal_state per signal is readable from InsightGraph.signal_results (confirmed in D-1 — _iter_signal_results already iterates these). No need for RootCauseV1 to get signal_state. Use InsightGraph signal rows for contributor sentence selection logic.
